title: oxalis reclinata var. quinata
intro: Oxalis reclinata var. quinata, commonly known as the Five-Leafed Wood Sorrel, is a plant species belonging to the Oxalidaceae family, characterized by its distinctive five-lobed leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its high levels of oxalic acid, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Oxalis reclinata var. quinata include its potential to aid in reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be used fresh in salads, highlighting its versatility in herbal preparations.
